error_signature: %warning-varhidden: <file>:<n>: declaration of signal hides declaration in upper scope: <ident> <n> | integer i; | ^ <file>:<n>: ... location of original declaration <n> | integer i; | ^ ... for warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/varhidden?v=<n>.<n> ... use "/* verilator lint_off varhidden */" and lint_on around source to disable this message. %warning-varhidden: <file>:<n>: declaration of signal hides declaration in upper scope: <ident> <n> | integer i; | ^ <file>:<n>: ... location of original declaration <n> | integer i; | ^ %error: <file>:<n>: can't find definition of variable: <ident> <n> | accum_reg <= '<n>; | ^~~~~~~~~ %error: exiting due to <n> error(s)
raw_error_example:
%Warning-VARHIDDEN: output/fir_filter.sv:69:17: Declaration of signal hides declaration in upper scope: 'i'
   69 |         integer i;
      |                 ^
                    output/fir_filter.sv:29:13: ... Location of original declaration
   29 |     integer i;
      |             ^
                    ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/VARHIDDEN?v=5.020
                    ... Use "/* verilator lint_off VARHIDDEN */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-VARHIDDEN: output/fir_filter.sv:112:17: Declaration of signal hides declaration in upper scope: 'i'
  112 |         integer i;
      |                 ^
                    output/fir_filter.sv:29:13: ... Location of original declaration
   29 |     integer i;
      |             ^
%Error: output/fir_filter.sv:96:13: Can't find definition of variable: 'accum_reg'
   96 |             accum_reg <= '0;
      |             ^~~~~~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
root_cause: The accumulator variable was named `accum` but referenced as `accum_reg` in the always block, and local integer declarations in generate blocks unnecessarily shadowed module-level variables causing warnings.
fix_rule: Use consistent variable names between declarations and all usages, and remove duplicate local integer declarations inside always blocks when a module-scope integer already exists.
